query: fluorescent green tinted hospital corridor cold light photograph
message: anti-aesthetic / color_and_tone / sickly_cast — fluorescent cold/green clinical tints, institutional cast

response.objective_caption: A long underground pedestrian corridor in a railway station features an arched white brick ceiling and pale yellow painted brick walls. A dark red signal fixture hangs from the ceiling in the foreground, below a white directional sign indicating Glasgow Platform and Platforms 2 and 3. A series of overhead fluorescent lights runs along the curved ceiling, illuminating the brown concrete floor and the receding tunnel. On the left side, a staircase with a red handrail leads upward. On the right wall, several framed posters and notices are mounted in a row above a blue waste bin. At the far end of the corridor, two small windows sit above a radiator.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A long underground pedestrian corridor in a railway station with an arched white brick ceiling and pale yellow painted brick walls showing extensive peeling paint, stains, and neglect. The overall color palette is muted and faded, rendered in dull institutional yellows and browns with low vibrancy. Overhead fluorescent lights cast flat, oppressive illumination across the empty passageway, enhancing the melancholic and isolating atmosphere. A dark red signal fixture hangs from the ceiling near a directional sign for Glasgow Platform. On the right, a blue bin sits on the worn concrete floor beneath several notices mounted on the deteriorating wall, while a staircase with a red handrail ascends on the left. The dirty surfaces and general state of disrepair evoke a strong sense of decay and atmospheric unease.
